LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOYCE MOVING AND STORAGE, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS A DUPLICATE ENTRY.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOYCE MOVING AND STORAGE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR VIOLATION OF PUBLIC UTILITIES CODE 5142. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. UPON CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT CONFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. SECTION 5142 PENALIZES HOUSEHOLD GOODS CARRIERS WHO FAIL TO RELEASE THE GOODS AFTER THE AMOUNT OF A LIEN HAS BEEN TENDERED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT ASSERTED A LIEN ON PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY OR THAT DEFENDANT FAILED TO RETURN THE PLAINTIFF'S GOODS AFTER PLAINTIFF TENDERED THE AMOUNT OF A LIEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT JOYCE MOVING AND STORAGE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSE OF ACTIONS FOR CONVERSION, NEGLIGENCE, AND BREACH OF CONTRACT ARE OVERRULED. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE PUBLIC UTILITIES CODE IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2015 Set For Trial On Jul-25-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Justin Draa of Greenfield Draa & Harrington LLP telephone number 408-995-5600 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff.
Gordon McAuley of Williams & Gumbiner LLP telephone number 415-686-5508 for Defendant/Moving Party.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Joyce Moving and Storage, Inc.'s Demurrer to Fourth Cause of Action of the Amended Complaint for violation of Public Utilities Code 5142 is sustained without leave to amend. Section 5142 penalizes household goods carriers who fail to release the goods after the amount of a lien has been tendered. Plaintiff fails to allege that defendant asserted a lien on plaintiff's property or that defendant failed to return the plaintiff's goods after plaintiff tendered the amount of a lien. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-14-2015 Continued To Dec-02-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-10-2015 Continued To Sep-30-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Maureen Harrington, of Greenfield Draa & Harrington LLP, Tel.408.995.5600, for plaintiffs;
Gordon D. McAuley, of Williams & Gumbiner LLP, Tel. 415.755.1880, for defendants;

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JOYCE MOVING AND STORAGE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the first, second, and third cause of actions for conversion, negligence, and breach of contract are overruled. A bailee/bailor relationship was created and the statute of limitations has not yet run. Where a bailee has lawfully acquired possession of a bailor's property, the statute of limitations does not begin to run until the bailee willfully fails to redeliver the bailor's property upon the latter's demand. Here, Defendant lawfully acquired Plaintiff's property and failed to return the property when Plaintiff requested the return in 2014. Thus, the statute of limitations began to run in 2014 and Plaintiff's claim is ripe. [See Bufano v. San Francisco (1965) 233 Cal.App.2d 61,70.] The fourth cause of action for violation of the Public Utilities Code is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. PUC section 5113 states: "[t]he transportation of used household goods and personal effects in any truck or trailer for compensation over any public highway in this state is a highly specialized type of truck transportation. This chapter is enacted for the limited purpose of providing necessary regulation for this specialized type of truck transportation only, and is not to be construed for any purpose as a precedent for the extension of such regulation to any other type of truck transportation not presently so restricted." Plaintiff has not alleged in the complaint that Defendant transported the household goods. Here, the gravamen of the harm is the storage of Plaintiff's property, therefore the Public Utilities Code does not apply. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H.
